message.content:
I need to understand a bug in a Storybook JSON editor addon. The bug is: "Unknown component type" error is displaying in the wrong (next?) JSON item in the editor.

Explore these files and explain how the JSON editor validates and displays errors:
1. .storybook/addons/json-editor/ - all files
2. src/services/sdui/renderer/ScreenRenderer.tsx
3. src/services/sdui/renderer/FallbackComponent.tsx
4. .storybook/addons/json-editor/sduiLinter.ts

I need to understand:
- How the linter reports errors and at what positions
- How FallbackComponent renders "Unknown component type" 
- How ScreenRenderer handles unknown types
- Whether the bug is in the linter position calculation or in the renderer's fallback display

content.text:
⊰IUTP

I'll explore these files to understand the JSON editor validation flow and identify where the "Unknown component type" error positioning bug occurs.
Now let me read the key files to understand the flow:

content.text: Let me now create a simple test case to trace the findJsonPath function behavior:
Excellent! I found the bug. Let me document this more clearly with additional test cases:

content.content:
=== DETAILED TRACE ===

Path: /body/0/type
  [key=body] Found at offset=20, setting pos=20
  [idx=0] Starting from pos=29 (arrStart=28)
  After loop: pos=70, currentIdx=0, char='
'
  [key=type] Found at offset=7, setting pos=77
Result: { from: 77, to: 110 }
Text: "\"type\": \"badType2\", \"props\": {} }"


Path: /body/1/type
  [key=body] Found at offset=20, setting pos=20
  [idx=1] Starting from pos=29 (arrStart=28)
  After loop: pos=113, currentIdx=0, char=']'
Result: null


=== WHY? ===
The issue:
1. For /body/0/type: correctly positions at 'type' in first item
2. For /body/1/type: fails to find second item properly
   - Loop terminates when currentIdx=0 after finding first '{' of second item
   - Then looks back with lastIndexOf('{', pos) but condition 'lastBrace >= arrStart' fails
   - Returns null

content.text: Now let me trace through what happens with the actual error reporting:

content.content:
Parsed body items:
  [0] type="heading"
  [1] type="badTypeUnknown"


Diagnostics generated:

[0] Unknown component type "heading"
    Path: /body/0/type
    Location found: true
    Positions: from=90, to=129
    Text at position: "\"type\": \"badTypeUnknown\", \"props\": {} }"
    Context: " },\n    { \"type\": \"badTypeUnknown\", \"props\": {} }\n  ]\n}"

[1] Unknown component type "badTypeUnknown"
    Path: /body/1/type
    Location found: false
    Positions: from=0, to=0


=== THE BUG ===
The 2nd error (badTypeUnknown) has position from=0, to=0
This causes CodeMirror to highlight the BEGINNING of the JSON
Instead of highlighting badTypeUnknown on line with index [1]

content.text: Perfect! Now I can see the complete picture. Let me create one more test showing the CASCADING effect:

content.content:
JSON with 3 unknown types in body:
 {
  "id": "test",
  "body": [
    { "type": "badType0", "props": {} },
    { "type": "badType1", "props": {} },
    { "type": "badType2", "props": {} }
  ]
}

  Line number: 5

Item [1]: type="badType1"
  Path searched: /body/1/type

  Positions: from=118, to=151
  Line number: 6
  Text at position: "\"type\": \"badType2\", "

Item [2]: type="badType2"
  Path searched: /body/2/type
  Location found: false
  ⚠️  FAILED - defaults to from=0, to=0 (beginning of file)


════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
Line 1: {  ← Error marker here (from=0)
Line 2:   "id": "test",
Line 3:   "body": [
Line 4:     { "type": "badType0", "props": {} },
Line 5:     { "type": "badType1", "props": {} },

Line 7:   ]
Line 8: }

content.text: Interesting! Now let me check the actual registry to understand what component types are valid:
Perfect! Now let me verify my understanding by checking one more thing - how the error display works in context:

content.content:
╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
║                      BUG: JSON Editor Error Position Bug                     ║
║          "Unknown component type" error displays on WRONG JSON item           ║
╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝

Test JSON:
 {
  "id": "vouchers",
  "body": [
    { "type": "heading", "props": { "text": "Select Voucher" } },
    { "type": "unknownType1", "props": {} },
    { "type": "unknownType2", "props": {} }
  ]
}

════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
STEP 1: Linter validates each body component
════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

Component [0]: type="heading"

Component [1]: type="unknownType1"

Component [2]: type="unknownType2"

════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
STEP 2: findJsonPath locates error positions
════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

Searching: /body/0/type
  Type value: "heading"
  ✓ Found at: from=106, to=144
  Snippet: ""type": "unknow..."

Searching: /body/1/type
  Type value: "unknownType1"
  ✓ Found at: from=151, to=188
  Snippet: ""type": "unknow..."

Searching: /body/2/type
  Type value: "unknownType2"
  ✗ FAILED - returns null
  CodeMirror will use fallback: from=0, to=0

════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
STEP 3: Errors displayed in CodeMirror editor
════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

Error [0]: Unknown component type "heading"
  ✓ Position: from=106, to=144
  Underline location: Line 5

Error [1]: Unknown component type "unknownType1"
  ✓ Position: from=151, to=188
  Underline location: Line 6

Error [2]: Unknown component type "unknownType2"
  ✗ Position: from=0, to=0
  Underline location: Line 1
  ⚠️  BUG: Points to beginning of file instead of line 6

════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
ROOT CAUSE ANALYSIS
════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

The bug is in findJsonPath() function in sduiLinter.ts, lines 41-86:

PROBLEM:
  When parsing array indices [2], [3], etc., the function's index-tracking
  logic increments currentIdx when it encounters a comma at bracketDepth=0.
  
  However, for index=2 (3rd item), the loop:
  1. Enters with idx=2, currentIdx=-1
  2. Finds '{' (bracketDepth becomes 1)
  3. Processes first object completely
  4. Finds ',' at bracketDepth=0 → increments to currentIdx=0
  5. Finds '{' (bracketDepth becomes 1) 
  6. Never finds another comma at bracketDepth=0 (still inside object)
  7. Loop exits with currentIdx=0, but we need currentIdx=2
  
  Then the fallback at line 69-72:
    if (currentIdx < idx - 1) {
      const lastBrace = doc.lastIndexOf('{', pos);
      if (lastBrace >= arrStart) pos = lastBrace;
    }
  
  This condition fails because currentIdx (0) < idx-1 (1) but doesn't find
  the correct object opening brace, returns null.

CONSEQUENCE:
  - findJsonPath returns null for body items [2] and beyond
  - Diagnostic defaults to { from: 0, to: 0 }
  - Error marker shows at beginning of JSON file, not at actual location
  - User sees "Unknown component type" error on the WRONG line

AFFECTED SCENARIOS:
  - Any JSON array with 3+ items where the 3rd+ item has an error
  - Cascading errors where error N+1 gets error N's position


════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
VISUAL BUG DEMONSTRATION
════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

What user sees in editor:

1 | { ← ERROR MARKER (wrong!)
2 |   "id": "vouchers",
3 |   "body": [
4 |     { "type": "heading", "props": { "text": "Select Voucher" } },
5 |     { "type": "unknownType1", "props": {} },
6 |     { "type": "unknownType2", "props": {} }
7 |   ]
8 | }
undefined
undefined
undefined
undefined
undefined
undefined
undefined
undefined

What user SHOULD see:

1 | {
2 |   "id": "vouchers",
3 |   "body": [ ← Error for 'unknownType1'
4 |     { "type": "heading", "props": { "text": "Select Voucher" } }, ← Error for 'unknownType2'
5 |     { "type": "unknownType1", "props": {} },
6 |     { "type": "unknownType2", "props": {} }
7 |   ]
8 | }
undefined
undefined
undefined
undefined
undefined
undefined
undefined
undefined
